source_paper_title: The Complexity of Recognizing Facets for the Knapsack Polytope
context:
Section 5 (Concluding Remarks), page 16 (PDF page 16/17)

It is still open whether it can be done in time f(K)· poly(n) where f is some computable function independent of n, i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.
title: Fixed-parameter tractability of knapsack facet recognition parameterized by the number of distinct coefficients
latex:
Let \(n\in\mathbb{N}\). Given \(a\in\mathbb{N}^n\) and \(b\in\mathbb{N}\), define the (0–1) knapsack set \(Q:=\{x\in\{0,1\}^n: a^\top x\le b\}\) and its knapsack polytope \(P:=\mathrm{conv}(Q)\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\). Given an integer inequality \(\alpha^\top x\le \beta\) with \((\alpha,\beta)\in\mathbb{Z}^{n+1}\), let \(K:=|\alpha|_+ := |\{\alpha_i: \alpha_i>0,\ i\in[n]\}|\) be the number of distinct positive coefficients in \(\alpha\). Define the decision problem \(\mathrm{KNAPSACK\_FACETS}\): decide whether \(\alpha^\top x\le \beta\) defines a facet of \(P\) (i.e., it is valid for \(P\), and the face \(F:=\{x\in P: \alpha^\top x=\beta\}\) has dimension \(\dim(P)-1\)).

Problem: Determine whether there exists a computable function \(f:\mathbb{N}\to\mathbb{N}\) and a polynomial \(p\) such that \(\mathrm{KNAPSACK\_FACETS}\) can be solved for all inputs in time \(f(K)\cdot p(n,\langle a,b,\alpha,\beta\rangle)\), i.e., whether \(\mathrm{KNAPSACK\_FACETS}\) is fixed-parameter tractable when parameterized by \(K=|\alpha|_+\).
open_problem_quote: It is still open whether it can be done in time f(K)· poly(n) where f is some computable function independent of n, i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
